description_long: This is a classic British red telephone booth, featuring a rectangular structure with a domed roof and prominent signage above the door. The booth is painted a vivid red, with multiple glass window panes set into the door and side panels, framed by red metal mullions. The front displays a white sign with the word 'TELEPHONE' in black capital letters, and a gold crown emblem above it, both of which are iconic features of the K6 telephone kiosk design. The door is hinged and includes a metallic handle, while the interior appears to contain a telephone unit, though details are partially obscured. The sides and rear of the booth also feature window panels, but the rear panel is solid with decorative rectangular embossing. The overall construction suggests a robust, weather-resistant structure suitable for outdoor urban environments.
description: A classic red British telephone booth with glass-paneled door and sides, white 'TELEPHONE' signage, and a domed roof with a gold crown emblem.
description_view_0: Front view showing the glass-paneled door, white 'TELEPHONE' sign, and gold crown emblem above the entrance.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the glass window panels and partial view of the interior telephone unit.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a solid red panel with rectangular embossing and the 'TELEPHONE' sign above.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view showing the glass window panels and a partial view of the door handle.
attribute_window_configuration: multiple rectangular glass panes on three sides, solid rear panel
attribute_signage_or_labeling: White 'TELEPHONE' sign with black letters above the door, gold crown emblem above signage
